subject: History
chapter: Mahatma Gandhi and the Nationalist Movement

remember: Students will be able to recall the key events and milestones in the life of Mahatma Gandhi.
Students will be able to identify the major figures and organizations involved in the nationalist movement.
Students will be able to list the main causes and consequences of the Non-Cooperation Movement.
Students will be able to describe the significance of the Salt March and the Dandi March.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of swaraj and its relevance to the nationalist movement.
Students will be able to analyze the role of non-violence and civil disobedience in the nationalist movement.
Students will be able to understand the impact of the nationalist movement on Indian society and politics.
Students will be able to describe the relationship between the nationalist movement and the Indian National Congress.
apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of non-violence and civil disobedience to real-life situations.
Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the nationalist movement.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of key figures and organizations in the nationalist movement.
Students will be able to design a plan for a peaceful protest or civil disobedience movement.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the causes and consequences of the nationalist movement.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of the nationalist movement on Indian politics and society.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the nationalist movement with other social and political movements in India.
Students will be able to identify the key challenges and limitations of the nationalist movement.

ncert_objectives: To understand the life and philosophy of Mahatma Gandhi and his role in the nationalist movement.
To analyze the causes and consequences of the Non-Cooperation Movement and the Salt March.
To evaluate the impact of the nationalist movement on Indian society and politics.
To understand the significance of the Indian National Congress in the nationalist movement.
board_exam_objectives: To explain the concept of swaraj and its relevance to the nationalist movement.
To describe the role of non-violence and civil disobedience in the nationalist movement.
To analyze the impact of the nationalist movement on Indian society and politics.
To evaluate the relationship between the nationalist movement and the Indian National Congress.
